text: AFX-Operated Bridge Loses $24.15 Million in USDC Exploit Onchain security firm Blockaid said it detected an attack on a cross-chain bridge operated by Arbitrum-based protocol AFX at 21:30 UTC on July 22, with about 24.15 million USDC stolen. USDC is a U.S. dollar-pegged stablecoin issued by Circle. The compromised bridge was operated independently by AFX and was not Arbitrum's official bridge. — link

text: Offchain: We're aware of a report of a bridge hack on Arbitrum and are investigating. We can confirm that the transaction in question originated from a third party protocol, and the Arbitrum native bridge has not been hacked or exploited in any way.

text: BLOCKAID'S EXPLOIT DETECTION SYSTEM HAS IDENTIFIED A USDC BRIDGE POSSIBLE EXPLOIT ON ARBITRUM: INFINITYHEDGE PeckShield: We detected an abnormal bridge withdrawal that extracted ~$24.15M USDC from an Arbitrum USDC custody bridge contract. The on-chain bridge logic was not bypassed; the malicious withdrawal was authorized by a quorum of hot-validator signatures and then finalized after the dispute period.

text: Senate Republicans Release Updated CLARITY Act Text, Ethics Rules Spark Bipartisan Dispute According to crypto journalist Eleanor Terrett, Senate Republicans released an updated CLARITY Act text, with the ethics package becoming the main point of dispute. The proposal would bar the president, vice president, members of Congress, federal judges and other covered officials from issuing or sponsoring paid digital assets while in office, and require them to sell crypto holdings or place them in a blind trust. It would also give the Department of Justice civil enforcement authority over violations, a move opposed by Democrats. The updated text keeps previous BRCA, stablecoin rewards and self-custody provisions, while adding new crypto crime enforcement measures, including more investigation funding, law enforcement training and a cyber center targeting nation-state threats. — link

text: Ethereum Validator Exit Queue Falls From Over 2.6M ETH in September 2025 to Zero, While 2.48M ETH Waits to Enter Staking According to Arkham, citing beaconcha in data, Ethereum's validator exit queue has fallen to zero, meaning unstaking no longer requires a wait. Meanwhile, around 2.48 million ETH is queued to enter staking, with an estimated wait of 43 days. The exit queue previously exceeded 2.6 million ETH in September 2025. About 40.9 million ETH is currently staked, representing 33.55% of total supply, across roughly 885,000 active validators, with an average APR of 2.64%. — link

text: SEC Commissioner Hester Peirce: Some Crypto Vaults and Onchain Lending Strategies May Fall Under Securities Laws SEC Commissioner Hester Peirce said whether crypto vaults and onchain lending strategies fall under federal securities laws depends on their specific structure and operations. Products involving managerial decisions over yield strategies, asset allocation, interest rates, loan-to-value limits or liquidation thresholds may raise securities, investment company or investment adviser issues, while some onchain loans may also have the characteristics of securities. Peirce encouraged market participants to engage with the SEC and provide feedback on whether existing rules should be updated to accommodate vaults and onchain lending. — link

text: Utility coins been cooking on Robinhood. Few coins which are interesting/main narratives: $WOOD (30 mil): An agentic trading platform. Idea is to enable any agent to manage an onchain fund with vaults, governed strategies and verifiable track record. Marketing and branding is cool, they have the full suite of optics with the co founders having good comms, product is still in testnet though. $PRIZE has a fully working product since day one and connects to actual Robinhood brokerage accounts and 50x lower mcap. Sherwood going higher is +ev to agentic trading protocols so I'm happy if it performs. $PONS (27 mil): Launchpad that's taken #1 volume after the death of Noxa. Nothing special stands out to me other than the Vlad follow. There will be a leading DEX but still feels too early to tell imo. $INDEX (12 mil): Tax ponzi token that was followed by Vlad. Their marketing and positioning is quite impressive. Unfortunately it's quite a slippery slope as volume disappears. Since vamps are so rampant I think staying in Ponzi's for more than a few days is quite dangerous and one of the bigger ticking time bombs in this meta game. $AI (6 mil): Artificial Inu is the most interesting coin I've seen. It's come from the Long xyz launchpad which pairs memes against stocks instead of ETH, Virtuals or a native pairing. PONS has a model where launchpad fees buyback & burn the PONS token itself. Long doesn't have a token or said they plan to, meaning all the volume flows back to holders of tokens from their launchpad. So far AI has resulted in 62% of NVDA's buys on Uniswap in the past 24 hours. It's quite an interesting flywheel and already paid out 270k in NVDA rewards. I've got a bag from around 5 mil, it's up a lot in the past 24 hours so tread carefully on entries. Issue I've had with tech plays is over IQ'ing narratives in the beginning, forgot when conditions are hot you need to dumb things down and ape obvious attention reflective tokens. Think that's why you see so many 3rd cycles get washed as it's the same games being played in slightly different form, wisdom can hinder short term trigger pulling. Zinc had a good point about fable being so advanced now that you can pump out tech slop tokens within and hour or two that would be on par with the 9-10 fig runners from AI szn if you're reasonably smart. Discernment will be significantly harder this time round if/when we get the next real tech szn. Interesting note is $JIMOTHY has gotten a strong bid and becoming significantly more viral in the past two days. Survived the first wave of attention. Feels like it's on the brink of hitting main stream but not quite there. The trade is assuming how close that gap it is. If it does go parabolic among normies and gets the Elon tweet then I think it has a low/medium chance of 9 figs, gut tells me it would get front run though, guess its dependant on how much pump have bundled/willing to crime. Cashcat and Ansem with small pull backs. Like I said before, people have been recovering from their onchain hangovers after all the liquidity vamps. Probably slower range until they break 100 mil and 300 mil respectively imo. Really isn't much liquidity when a couple low 8 fig coins put dips in the leading memes. BTC silently climbing in the midst of one of the better onchain runs we've had in a while. If it breaks 67k I'd be looking for quite a fun week post break. If history is anything to go off, onchain has always bottomed a month or so before majors. Let's pray this time's no different.

text: South Korea's Five Major Crypto Exchanges See Daily Volume Drop 89% as KOSPI Rises 114.44% Average daily trading volume across South Korea's five major crypto exchanges — Upbit, Bithumb, Coinone, Korbit and Gopax — fell about 89% year-on-year, from $2.82 billion in July 2025 to $305 million. Over the same period, the KOSPI rose 114.44%. ZDNet Korea also reported that daily volume across the five exchanges was down 88% on Monday, while weaker fee income prompted some platforms to sell crypto holdings. Korbit sold 15 BTC and 60 ETH, raising about KRW 1.6 billion ($1 million). — link

text: Zilliqa Ledger App Flaw Exposes Private Keys, Upbit Flags ZIL as a Cautionary Asset Zilliqa disclosed a critical nonce-generation flaw in its Ledger app that allows private keys to be recovered from public signatures after about five native transactions. The issue affected all versions released from 2019 to 2026, with active exploitation observed on July 19. Native transactions have been suspended; affected keys must be retired, while EVM transactions are unaffected. Upbit subsequently designated ZIL as a cautionary asset across its KRW and BTC markets. Deposits and withdrawals remain suspended, and trading support may be terminated if the issue is not resolved. — link

text: S&P Dow Jones and Pantera Launch Fundamental-Based Digital Asset Index, Top Holdings Include ETH, BNB and SOL S&P Dow Jones Indices and Pantera Capital have launched the S&P Pantera Digital Asset Index, designed to provide institutional investors with a more disciplined and structured benchmark for digital asset allocation. Unlike traditional crypto indexes that track price momentum or popular tokens, the index uses a rules-based approach similar to traditional financial benchmarks, including only tokens and projects with real-world applications and actual revenue generation. The index currently includes 18 constituent tokens. While the full list has not been fully disclosed, the top five confirmed holdings are ETH, BNB, SOL, TRX, and HYPE. — link
